sections.paragraph:
fut. יֶחֱשׂף a kindred root to חָסַף (which see)-

(1) to strip off the bark, as of a tree. (Arab. حسف and سحف). Joe 1:7.

(2) to strip off a covering, followed by an acc. of the covering. Isa 47:2, חֶשְׂפִּי שֹׁבֶל “strip off the train.” Jer 13:26, with acc. of person, to make bare or naked, the covering being stripped off. Jer 49:10 Isa 52:10, “the Lord has made bare his holy arm.” Eze 4:7 Isa 20:4, חֲשׂוּפַי שֵׁת “with the buttocks uncovered.” To make a tree bare, i.q. to strip off its leaves, Psa 29:8.

(3) to draw (as water), properly from the surface, oben abfchöpfen, Isa 30:4 Hagg. 2:16 . (In Arabic خَسُوفُ is a perennial well of water in sandy ground; but the derivation of this word is to be sought elsewhere [from خسف to let down]).

Derivatives, חֲשִׂיף, מַחְשׂף and pr.n. חֲשׂוּפָא.

[חָשִׂף see חָשִׂיף .]
